# Your Name | Profile Links

> Your Name is fictional placeholder data included in this starter. The site collects example social, portfolio, and contact links in one clear profile page.

Site: https://example.com
Language: en
Author: Your Name
Category: Personal Website

## Profile

- Name: Your Name
- Domain Label: profile-links-starter
- Role: Product designer, frontend tinkerer | and content creator.
- Bio: This example starter shows how to organize one profile links site around editable config files, reusable legal content, and static-friendly SEO defaults.

## Profile Facts

- Focus: Design systems + frontend
- Based in: New York, USA

- recruiters

- clients

## Search Intents

- find official profile links
- verify social accounts
- discover work and contact options

## About This Starter

This repository is an upstream starter for profile-link sites. It is designed to be forked, edited through JSON files, and reused with placeholder-safe example data.

## Forking Notes

- What to replace: Update the placeholder identity, links, contact details, legal copy, and images before publishing.
- Where to edit: Most forks only need config/site.json, config/profile.json, config/links.json, config/llms.json, config/legal/*.json, and public/images/.

## Extra Q&A

- Q: Can I reuse this starter for my own profile or brand?
  A: Yes. Fork it, replace the placeholder content, and adjust the legal and SEO settings for your own deployment.
- Q: Which files usually need edits first?
  A: Most forks start with config/site.json, config/profile.json, config/links.json, config/llms.json, config/legal/*.json, and public/images/.

## FAQ

- Q: Who is "Your Name"?
  A: "Your Name" is placeholder data used to show how the starter works before you replace the content with your own profile.
- Q: What is this site for?
  A: It is a clean single-page link hub for social profiles, portfolio destinations, and contact information.
- Q: How do I customize it?
  A: Edit the JSON files in the config folder, replace the example legal text, and deploy the generated site to GitHub Pages or your own domain.
